terms.definition: A clock that can be set to ring or buzz at a chosen time to wake someone up.
terms.exampleUsage: Neha set her alarm clock to ring at 6 o'clock every morning.

terms.definition: Arriving or doing something at the expected or correct time; not late.
terms.exampleUsage: After getting an alarm clock, Neha became very punctual for school.

terms.definition: The early morning time when the sun begins to rise and light appears in the sky.
terms.exampleUsage: The birds start singing at dawn when the sky turns pink and orange.

terms.definition: When a flower opens its petals and shows its full beauty; to blossom.
terms.exampleUsage: The flowers bloom in the morning and fill the garden with colour.

terms.definition: A group of words arranged in order that makes complete sense. It starts with a capital letter and ends with a full stop, question mark, or exclamation mark.
terms.exampleUsage: 'Neha wakes up early.' is a complete sentence.

terms.definition: The part of a sentence that tells us who or what the sentence is about.
terms.exampleUsage: In the sentence 'The bird sings,' the subject is 'The bird.'

terms.definition: The part of a sentence that tells us what the subject does or is.
terms.exampleUsage: In the sentence 'Neha wakes up early,' the predicate is 'wakes up early.'

terms.definition: Words that have the same ending sound. Poems often use rhyming words to sound musical.
terms.exampleUsage: In poems, words like 'sun' and 'fun' rhyme because they end with the same sound.

terms.definition: A gentle, light wind that feels pleasant on the skin.
terms.exampleUsage: The cool morning breeze made Neha feel fresh after she woke up.

terms.definition: A set of activities that you do regularly in the same order, like your morning habits.
terms.exampleUsage: Neha's morning routine includes waking up, brushing her teeth, and having breakfast.
